hypothesis: E639-E646's shared next_step field repeats a copy-pasted claim: 'E620's coverage-aware component/property closure recommendation remains the next untried lever.' Reading E620's own entry and the full E621-E638 matrix history first falsifies this: slot_coverage_close_decode_weight (TwoTowerModel._slot_coverage_close_bias) IS that recommendation, built and iterated on across eighteen prior sessions (E621-E638) before the lineage deliberately pivoted to a different, PR#625-inspired lever (required_slot_margin_decode_weight, E639) which E639 itself frames as 'a more concrete framing' of the same E620 idea. Filing another closure-mechanism experiment would duplicate E621-E638. Instead this session picks the other item flagged as open across the same six iterations: 'a genuinely powered multi-seed/retrained comparison of required_slot_margin_decode_weight remains open (E626/E639's own deferral, still not picked up by E627-E646).' Hypothesis under test: does required_slot_margin_decode_weight=2's previously-reported net-positive effect (E639, E641, E645, E646, all on the same seed-0 checkpoint) replicate across independently-trained same-recipe checkpoints (different seeds)?

prior_lever_status_confirmed.e620_recommendation: coverage-aware closure across component and property boundaries, with role-correctness preserved
prior_lever_status_confirmed.outcome: partially retained default-off (v73-v75, E635-E637 positive mixed evidence: first strict-v2 fix, Modal reachability, nested family accounting), then E638's root-slot-coverage gate rejected as a capacity-limited dead end; lineage pivoted to required_slot_margin_decode_weight (E639) as an explicit reframing of the same recommendation, not an alternative to it
prior_lever_status_confirmed.conclusion: the next_step boilerplate in E639/E640/E641/E642/E643/E644/E645/E646 is stale copy-paste, not an accurate description of an untried lever; corrected here

seed0_reused.note: reused verbatim from E645/E646, byte-identical, no retrain

eval_recipe.flags: honest_slot_contract, slot_contract_constrained_decode, slot_contract_in_context, semantic_role_contract_in_context, semantic_role_decode_weight=8, slot_coverage_close_decode_weight=2, schema_value_decode_weight=4, schema_opaque_close_decode_weight=4, schema_role_slot_decode_weight=8, semantic_plan_decode_weight=4, semantic_plan_margin_decode_weight=2, semantic_plan_binding_decode_weight=1, semantic_plan_root_decode_weight=8, semantic_plan_root_margin_decode_weight=2, semantic_plan_repeated_array_close_margin_decode_weight=2, semantic_plan_repeated_slot_margin_decode_weight=2, semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight=2, semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight=2, grammar_ltr_max_tokens=160
eval_recipe.discarded_interrupted_attempts: seed1 margin2 and seed2 margin0 each had one initial attempt cut off mid-suite by an unrelated shell/tool timeout (missing eval_smoke.json); both were deleted (rm -rf) and re-run to full completion inside the 3-minute cap before being used as evidence, per this repo's 'a timed-out, interrupted, or killed run is never evidence' rule

pooled_3seed_statistics_n57.method: slm_training.evals.power_protocol.bootstrap_paired_ci (57 records treated i.i.d., replicating E642's own method) and cluster_bootstrap_ci (57 records resampled by seed-cluster, k=3 clusters of 19), 2000 resamples, seed=0, alpha=0.05

verdict: The previously-reported single-checkpoint net-positive effect of required_slot_margin_decode_weight=2 (E639, E641, E645, E646, all on the same seed-0 checkpoint) does not replicate across two independently-trained same-recipe checkpoints. Per-seed binary meaningful_program_v1 delta flips sign between seeds (+1/-1/-1 out of 19); pooled 3-seed statistics show no seed-cluster-robust effect on meaningful_program_v1, reward_score, placeholder_fidelity, or component_type_recall (all CIs cross zero). structural_similarity is the one metric with a seed-cluster-robust positive delta (CI excludes zero in both naive and cluster bootstrap), a real but narrow finding. Three seeds is still thin (not DEFAULT_MIN_SUITE_N-powered), but is enough to overturn the accumulated single-checkpoint 'consistent gain' narrative. Not a ship claim; no checkpoint trained, promoted, or synced; default required_slot_margin_decode_weight (0.0) unchanged; no source code touched this session (clean tree throughout), so no version_stamp component bump applies.
decision: Do not promote or recommend enabling required_slot_margin_decode_weight based on prior single-checkpoint evidence. Keep it default-off (unchanged). Correct the lineage's own next_step boilerplate: E620's coverage-aware component/property closure recommendation is the already-explored E621-E638 arc, not an untried lever -- this should stop being repeated verbatim in future next_step fields. If required_slot_margin_decode_weight is pursued further, use 5+ seeds and/or a genuinely powered n>=20-per-suite sample before any promotion claim; structural_similarity's seed-robust gain is worth an isolated follow-up.
next_step: 1) required_slot_margin_decode_weight's apparent single-checkpoint benefit does not replicate across seeds on meaningful_program_v1/reward/fidelity/component_recall; a promotion case for it needs 5+ seeds or a >=20-per-suite powered sample, not more single-checkpoint traces. 2) structural_similarity's seed-cluster-robust positive delta (+0.0713, CI [0.0480, 0.0920]) is unexplained and worth a dedicated trace: is this a real structural benefit, or an artifact correlated with the same over-stuffing/padding behavior E643/E644 found gaming meaningful_program_v1's literal empty-children check? 3) The residual variadic-frame bind-reference class E646 found benign should still be watched if the lever's default is ever revisited. 4) Stop repeating 'E620's coverage-aware component/property closure recommendation remains the next untried lever' in future next_step fields -- it is factually established as already-tried (E621-E638) by this entry.
